title: TypeScript Fundamentals Quiz
description: Test your TypeScript type system knowledge.

questions.question: What is the difference between 'interface' and 'type' in TypeScript?

options.text: They are completely identical with no differences
Interfaces can be merged (declaration merging) and extended; types can use unions and intersections more flexibly
Types are for primitives only; interfaces are for objects only

questions.question: What does the ? operator mean when used on an interface property (e.g., role?: string)?

options.text: The property is optional — it may or may not be present

questions.question: What is a union type in TypeScript?

options.text: A type that combines all properties of two types
A type that can be one of several specified types
A type that excludes certain values
